2604.12948.title: Drawing on Memory: Dual-Trace Encoding Improves Cross-Session Recall in LLM Agents
2604.12948.paper_summary: LLM agents with persistent memory store information as flat factual records, providing little context for temporal reasoning, change tracking, or cross-session aggregation. Inspired by the drawing effect [3], we introduce dual-trace memory encoding. In this method, each stored fact is paired with a concrete scene trace, a narrative reconstruction of the moment and context in which the information was learned. The agent is forced to commit to specific contextual details during encoding, creating richer, more distinctive memory traces. Using the LongMemEval-S benchmark (4,575 sessions, 100 recall questions), we compare dual-trace encoding against a fact-only control with matched coverage and format over 99 shared questions. Dual-trace achieves 73.7% overall accuracy versus 53.5%, a +20.2 percentage point (pp) gain (95% CI: [+12.1, +29.3], bootstrap p < 0.0001). Gains concentrate in temporal reasoning (+40pp), knowledge-update tracking (+25pp), and multi-session aggregation (+30pp), with no benefit for single-session retrieval, consistent with encoding specificity theory [8]. Token analysis shows dual-trace encoding achieves this gain at no additional cost. We additionally sketch an architectural design for adapting dual-trace encoding to coding agents, with preliminary pilot validation.
2604.12948.paper_summary_zh: LLM 代理人具有持久記憶，將信息儲存為平面事實記錄，對於時間推理、變化追蹤或跨會話聚合提供的上下文很少。受到繪圖效應 [3] 的啟發，我們引入了雙重追蹤記憶編碼。在這種方法中，每個儲存的事實都與具體的場景追蹤配對，這是一種對學習信息的時刻和上下文的敘述重建。代理人在編碼過程中被迫承諾具體的上下文細節，從而創造出更豐富、更獨特的記憶痕跡。使用 LongMemEval-S 基準（4,575 次會話，100 個回憶問題），我們將雙重追蹤編碼與僅包含事實的對照組進行比較，兩者在 99 個共享問題上具有匹配的覆蓋率和格式。雙重追蹤的整體準確率達到 73.7%，而僅事實的對照組為 53.5%，增幅為 +20.2 個百分點（pp）（95% CI: [+12.1, +29.3]，自助法 p < 0.0001）。增益主要集中在時間推理（+40pp）、知識更新追蹤（+25pp）和多會話聚合（+30pp）上，對於單會話檢索沒有益處，這與編碼特異性理論 [8] 一致。標記分析顯示，雙重追蹤編碼在不增加額外成本的情況下實現了這一增益。我們還勾勒出一個將雙重追蹤編碼適應於編碼代理人的架構設計，並進行了初步的試點驗證。
2604.12948.author: Benjamin Stern et.al.
